* Fix CI: CORS+skipNegotiation for SignalR, .mud-select-input click for MudSelect, Cart hubUrl, @ignore for @wip scenarios

Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: two E2E CI failures — MudSelect click target and Wolverine SignalR injection

Failure 1 (CheckoutPage.cs): SelectAddressByNicknameAsync timed out (30s) because
AddressSelect.Locator('.mud-select-input').ClickAsync() targeted an element that
Playwright's hit-test could not reach in headless Chromium (MudBlazor renders a
transparent overlay on top of .mud-select-input). Reverted to AddressSelect.ClickAsync()
(outer wrapper), which was proven to open the dropdown reliably in the prior CI run.

Failure 2 (CheckoutFlowStepDefinitions.cs + OrderConfirmation.razor):
InvokeMessageAndWaitAsync timed out because OrderStatusChanged and ShipmentStatusChanged
have no local Wolverine handlers — they are SignalR-transport-only messages. Wolverine's
TrackedSession waits for a handler terminal state that never fires. Replaced both calls
with direct IHubContext<StorefrontHub>.SendAsync('ReceiveMessage', ...) injection using a
CloudEvents-shaped payload matching Wolverine's transport format.

Latent bug also fixed: OrderConfirmation.razor's OnSseEvent handler was looking for
'NewStatus' and 'TrackingNumber' (PascalCase) in JsonElement. SignalR serializes via
System.Text.Json camelCase defaults, so properties arrive as 'newStatus'/'trackingNumber'.
This was hidden because InvokeMessageAndWaitAsync always timed out before any message
reached the browser.

* fix: MudSelect Force=true on .mud-select-input, split timeouts to 15s/10s, update docs

Four CI failures all shared the same root cause: SelectAddressByNicknameAsync.
AddressSelect.ClickAsync() (outer wrapper) clicked the bounding-box centre which
landed on the <label> element in headless Chromium — MudBlazor received no open
event, so [role='listbox'] never appeared within 10 seconds.

Fix: Force=true on .mud-select-input bypasses Playwright's hit-test (which was
blocked by MudBlazor's transparent .mud-input-mask overlay) and dispatches the
click directly to the element carrying MudBlazor's @onclick handler.

Also:
- Split MudSelectPopoverTimeoutMs into MudSelectListboxTimeoutMs (15s) and
  MudSelectOptionTimeoutMs (10s) — two distinct wait phases deserve distinct constants
- Updated Pattern 3 and Pitfall 4 in e2e-playwright-testing.md with full
  three-failure-mode history and Force=true rationale

commit.message:
refactor: centralize autosave trigger, move undo/redo to execute_actions

Three changes:

1. Autosave is now triggered once at the end of handle_key() via
   autosave_if_dirty() — checks if buffer is dirty after all actions
   are processed. Removes schedule_autosave() from translate_vim_action.
   This covers edits, undo, redo, task toggle, and any future mutation
   without each handler needing to remember.

2. Action::Undo/Redo handled in execute_actions() alongside other
   editor mutations, not passed through to the TUI.
   handle_vim_command() now just returns the action intent.

3. Tests updated to verify buffer content (the observable behavior)
   rather than action return values (implementation detail).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add hexagonal port traits for browser automation (M2)

Define 6 port traits (BrowserPort, PagePort, ElementPort, NetworkPort,
EmulationPort, InputPort) with framework-agnostic signatures using
standard Rust types. Implement all ports on existing CDP types
(Browser, Page, Element).

Port traits decouple consumers from the concrete CDP implementation,
enabling testing with mocks and future backend swaps. All traits are
object-safe for dynamic dispatch via &dyn.

Closes #169, #170, #171, #172, #173, #174

commit.message:
cfl: rewrite icc_tiffdump_fuzzer V3 for coverage breakthrough

Complete rewrite with 2-phase architecture:

Phase 1 — In-memory libtiff:
- Read ALL TIFF tags (planarconfig, photometric, sampleformat, etc.)
- Pixel data via TIFFReadEncodedStrip() + TIFFReadScanline()
- ICC profile extraction + deep exercise (Validate, tag iteration, Describe)
- Multi-directory TIFF support

Phase 2 — CTiffImg file-based:
- Open(), ReadLine(), GetPhoto(), GetIccProfile()
- Exercises contiguous + separated planar paths
- Exercises all 6 photometric type mappings

OOM hardening:
- Profile size guard (256MB cap, 1KB minimum for tag exercise)
- Per-tag size/offset bounds checking vs declared profile size
- MPE amplification guard (tSize * 1024 > profileSize)
- 64-tag iteration cap, 64KB Describe() output cap

Coverage: 7,896 → 9,203 edges (+16.6%) in 2 minutes.

Dictionary: 79 new entries for CTiffImg code paths (planarconfig,
photometric types, sample formats, compression, resolution tags).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  role (ECR + SSM) and API task role (S3 + SSM); update CI user policy
  to ECS deploy permissions (RegisterTaskDefinition + UpdateService)
- Update rds.tf / redis.tf: security groups now reference ECS tasks SG
  instead of EKS node group SG
- Update ssm.tf: add thumbnail_bucket_name and redis_connection_string
  SSM parameters so ECS can inject them at task start
- Update vpc.tf: remove EKS-specific subnet discovery tags
- Update variables.tf: replace EKS node vars with ECS Fargate capacity
  vars (cpu, memory, desired_count, max_count per service) + TLS vars

commit.message:
Fix build system: add dl_find_object stub for musl compatibility

This commit resolves three CRITICAL audit findings:

1. Build system failure for Phase 0 validation (cmd/wain)
2. Inconsistent static linking across demonstration binaries
3. Missing wayland-demo binary

Root Cause:
-----------
GCC 14+ libgcc_eh.a references _dl_find_object (glibc 2.35+), which
musl libc does not provide. This caused link failures when building
with musl-gcc and -static flags.

Solution:
---------
- Created internal/render/dl_find_object_stub.c with weak stub that
  returns -1, triggering libgcc_eh's fallback frame discovery path
- Updated Makefile to compile stub and link it with all CGO binaries
- Simplified pure-Go demo builds (demo, wayland-demo, x11-demo) to
  use CGO_ENABLED=0 for guaranteed static linking
- Added stub cleanup to 'make clean' target

Verification:
-------------
- All binaries now build successfully: wain, demo, wayland-demo,
  x11-demo, widget-demo, gen-atlas
- All binaries are statically linked (verified with 'file' command)
- All Go tests pass (29 packages tested)
- No code complexity regressions (go-stats-generator diff)
- Actually improved: xdg.HandleEvent complexity decreased 60%

Files Modified:
---------------
- Makefile: Added DL_STUB_SRC/OBJ variables, stub build target,
  updated all demo build targets, updated clean target
- AUDIT.md: Marked 3 CRITICAL findings as [x] fixed
- internal/render/dl_find_object_stub.c: New stub implementation

commit.message:
fix: use $(SUFFIX) in version-check binary path for debug builds (#8148)

The version-check step in `build-jaeger` hardcoded the binary name
without `$(SUFFIX)`, so when invoked via
`_build-platform-binaries-debug` (which sets `DEBUG_BINARY=1` →
`SUFFIX=-debug`), the check either failed with "binary not found" or
silently validated the release binary instead of the debug one.

- **`scripts/makefiles/BuildBinaries.mk`**: Replace
`./cmd/jaeger/jaeger-$(GOOS)-$(GOARCH)` with
`./cmd/jaeger/jaeger$(SUFFIX)-$(GOOS)-$(GOARCH)` in both version-check
invocations — no-op for release builds (`SUFFIX` is empty), correct for
debug builds (`SUFFIX=-debug`).

commit.message:
fix: Node 24+ ESM import crash - lazy load copilot-sdk + postinstall patch

Fixes critical bug where 'squad init' crashed on Node 24.11.1 with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND
for vscode-jsonrpc/node (missing .js extension).

TWO-LAYER FIX:

Layer 1 (primary): Lazy imports in cli-entry.ts
- Changed eager top-level imports to dynamic imports
- Commands like 'init', 'status', 'migrate' no longer trigger copilot-sdk loading
- Only shell commands load copilot-sdk when actually needed
- Replaced VERSION import with local getPackageVersion() resolver

Layer 2 (backup): Postinstall patch script
- Created scripts/patch-esm-imports.mjs to fix broken import at install time
- Patches @github/copilot-sdk session.js to add .js extension
- Searches multiple locations (workspace hoisting, global install)
- Added to package.json postinstall hook and files array

ROOT CAUSE:
@github/copilot-sdk@0.1.32 has inconsistent ESM imports:
- session.js: from 'vscode-jsonrpc/node' ← BROKEN (no .js)
- client.js: from 'vscode-jsonrpc/node.js' ← correct

Node 24+ enforces strict ESM resolution. vscode-jsonrpc has no exports field.

VERIFICATION:
✅ 'squad init' works without copilot-sdk (instant, no crash)
✅ 'squad --version' works (zero dependencies)
✅ 'squad status' works (lazy-loads squad-sdk)
✅ 'squad' shell works (lazy-loads copilot-sdk, patch applied)
✅ All REPL UX E2E tests pass (22/22)
✅ Build succeeds (0 TypeScript errors)

commit.message:
fix: Correct registry key in apply_masternode_reg; add TLS to WS server; refresh tier on re-announce

- masternode_registry.rs: apply_masternode_reg now uses IP-only key (not
  IP:port) to match the convention used by startup registration and
  handle_masternode_announcement. Previously this created a duplicate
  registry entry so RegistrationSource::OnChain was never set, meaning
  on-chain-registered nodes were treated as transient Free-tier nodes
  and deleted on disconnect.

- masternode_registry.rs: RegistrationSource::OnChain is now reliably
  set because nodes.get_mut(&address) finds the correct entry.

- main.rs (announcement task): Re-broadcast loop now fetches the live
  registry entry each cycle so a Free-tier placeholder that is later
  upgraded via on-chain registration re-announces with the correct tier
  (Bronze/Silver/Gold) instead of repeating the stale Free tier forever.

- main.rs (auto-detect fallback): Improved warning message when
  collateral UTXO is not yet confirmed, explaining the node will
  upgrade automatically once the on-chain registration is processed.
  Added tip to set tier= explicitly in time.conf to skip auto-detect.

- config.rs, rpc/websocket.rs, main.rs: Wire up TLS acceptor for the
  WebSocket server (wss://). Reads wstls/wstlscert/wstlskey from
  time.conf; defaults to self-signed cert with wstls=1. Clients
  connecting with the wallet's AcceptAnyCerts verifier work without
  a CA.

- config.rs, message_handler.rs: Fix two pre-existing clippy lints
  (writeln_empty_string, cloned_ref_to_slice_refs).

Co-authored-by: rnwood <1327895+rnwood@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: exclude System.ServiceModel.* from CmdletsLoadContext to fix FaultException type identity conflict

The broad Default.Resolving handler loads System.ServiceModel.Primitives (which
contains FaultException<T>) into CmdletsLoadContext, while Cmdlets.dll (loaded
as a NestedModule into the DEFAULT ALC) resolves it from PS7's installation
directory. The same-version assembly in two different ALCs creates different type
identities, causing `catch (FaultException<OrganizationServiceFault>)` in
SetDataverseEntityMetadataCmdlet to silently miss the thrown exception. Entity
creation always fails with a propagated FaultException.

Fix: exclude System.ServiceModel.* from both the Default.Resolving handler and
CmdletsLoadContext.Load() so all code paths share PS7's copy of these assemblies
from the DEFAULT ALC. Dataverse SDK assemblies (Microsoft.Xrm.Sdk, etc.) are
still isolated in CmdletsLoadContext for Azure Functions compatibility.

Co-authored-by: rnwood <1327895+rnwood@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove System.ServiceModel exclusion from Default.Resolving, keep only in CmdletsLoadContext.Load

The previous commit's System.ServiceModel.* exclusion from Default.Resolving broke
module loading on Ubuntu PS7 builds that do NOT ship WCF assemblies. When PS7
does not have System.ServiceModel.Primitives in its installation, the only path to
find it is Default.Resolving loading it from cmdlets/net8.0/ into CmdletsLoadContext.
Excluding it from Default.Resolving caused FileNotFoundException on every module
import in those environments.

Correct approach:
- Default.Resolving: handles System.ServiceModel.* normally (loads from
  cmdlets/net8.0/ into CmdletsLoadContext when PS7 doesn't have them).
- CmdletsLoadContext.Load(): returns null for System.ServiceModel.*, falling back
  to DEFAULT ALC. This ensures one consistent copy regardless of platform:
  * PS7 ships them (Windows): DEFAULT ALC has PS7's copy; CmdletsLoadContext.Load
    returns null so ServiceClient also uses PS7's copy. TYPE MATCH.
  * PS7 doesn't ship them (Ubuntu): Default.Resolving loaded them into
    CmdletsLoadContext; DEFAULT ALC caches that resolution. CmdletsLoadContext.Load
    returns null so ServiceClient falls back to DEFAULT ALC which returns the
    same CmdletsLoadContext instance. TYPE MATCH.

Co-authored-by: rnwood <1327895+rnwood@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct comment tense in CmdletsLoadContext.Load System.ServiceModel exclusion

Co-authored-by: rnwood <1327895+rnwood@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: preload Microsoft.Crm.Sdk.Proxy into DEFAULT ALC at module import for PS type resolution

PS7's type resolver searches AssemblyLoadContext.Default.Assemblies. Microsoft.Crm.Sdk.Proxy.dll
(containing WhoAmIRequest, PublishXmlRequest, etc.) was only loaded into CmdletsLoadContext
as an internal dependency of ServiceClient. That loading happens entirely within CmdletsLoadContext
via CmdletsLoadContext.Load(), and never fires Default.Resolving, so the assembly never appears
in AssemblyLoadContext.Default.Assemblies. As a result, PS scripts using:
  New-Object Microsoft.Crm.Sdk.Messages.WhoAmIRequest
  [Microsoft.Crm.Sdk.Messages.PublishXmlRequest]
would fail with "Cannot find type" on Windows PS latest.

Fix: in OnImport(), after setting up Default.Resolving, explicitly call
AssemblyLoadContext.Default.LoadFromAssemblyPath() for Microsoft.Crm.Sdk.Proxy.dll. This
loads it directly into DEFAULT ALC, making its types available to PS's type resolver.

CmdletsLoadContext.Load() now also returns null for Microsoft.Crm.Sdk.Proxy so that
ServiceClient's internal usage falls back to the same DEFAULT ALC copy, maintaining a
single type-identity instance (no duplicate-ALC issues for request/response types).

* test: add AzureFunctionsCompatibilityTests E2E test demonstrating issue #168 fix

Adds AzureFunctionsCompatibilityTests.cs which simulates the Azure Functions PS 7.4
worker environment by pre-loading Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions v6.0.11
(from Azure Functions Core Tools worker dir) into DEFAULT ALC before importing the
module. This reproduces the exact mechanism of issue #168.

Key facts:
- Azure Functions PS 7.4 worker ships MELA v6.0.11 (AssemblyVersion=6.0.0.0)
- Module requires v8.0.0.0
- Stable module (v2.20.0): Default.Resolving throws FormatException parsing "8.0.13+eba..."
  → returns null → .NET uses DEFAULT ALC v6.x → FileLoadException (0x80131621)
- PR module: strips "+eba..." suffix → parses "8.0.13" → routes to CmdletsLoadContext
  → loads v8.x in isolation → success

Also adds CI workflow step to install Azure Functions Core Tools before E2E tests
so the new test runs automatically in CI.

Demo output (stable vs PR):
  Stable v2.20.0: FAILED: FileLoadException 0x80131621 (issue reproduced)
  PR module:      SUCCESS: Connection established (issue resolved)
